12.content: Ok, which girl would you like to grow?
12.title: grow

choices: Sakura get's held up by Naruto
121.content:
"Mom! I'm heading out!"

The owner of this voice was none other than the hidden leaf villages' very own giant kunochi, Sakura Haruno. She was a very large girl standing at approximately 52ft in height and weighed just about 40tons.

The pink-haired girl smiled confidently as she walked through the large tent built beside her parent's home that served as her room. She hadn't been able to fit in the house since she turned eight.

"Alright honey, you have a nice day!" her mother smiled.

"Yeah, what your mother said!" her father added.

"Don't worry, I will." the giantess giggled before carefully making her way down the street. Each step she took caused a small tremor on the ground and the citizens of the hidden leaf knew that was their cue to start making way.

"Attention all civilians of the hidden leaf village! Make way, Sakura Haruno is coming through!" someone announced via megaphone. As if they didn't already know to start giving the giant some space.

Sakura thanked them all kindly and they in tern smiled and greeted her back. She took extra care around people as the buildings to make sure she didn't accidently destroy anything. At her size it'd be so easy to make such a mistake too.

To her the village was like a model city used in films for a sense of scale. The only difference being that these were really bricks, real glass, and real people. So she would endeavor to be as careful as humanly possible.

As continued her trek to the academy she felt something settle on her shoulder. "Good morning Sakura."

She turned her head slightly, her emerald orbs settling on a purple-clad blonde sitting comfortably. "Good morning Ino."

The now identified girl smirked at her friend/rival's greeting. "So they actually let you graduate? What a surprise."

"Those classes are ancient history. We're both ninja now and we're both equal. I won't loose to you again." Sakura smirked back.

Ino didn't seem all to affected by the giant's declaration. "Hmph! We'll see."

With their peace's said, the two continued on to the academy.
121.title: Sakura the giant kunoichi

1211.content:
It didn't take long for the pair to reach their destination as Sakura could pretty much clear any gap in a matter of seconds. The old building itself stood slightly taller than her and was of course to small for her to even attempt to enter. Because of this she'd have to wait outside by one of the windows like always.

"This is my stop. See soon billboard brow." Ino teased before leaping off her rival's shoulders and onto the roof. The pinkette ignored blonde's attempt to get a rise out of her and 'hmphed' before walking to the side of the building and taking a seat next to an open window.

Her arrival of course drew the attention of her fellow classmates and a few of them waved while others were content to just ignore her. She didn't really care too much about that as she did the boy in blue. Sasuke Uchiha was his name, and he was the boy who was currently the object of her affections. Seeing him made her blush and she wished she could just shrink down and sit right next to him instead of that annoying blonde in the orange umpsuit.

Naruto Uzumaki, the class clown and boy who didn't know when to quit. For the last four years Naruto had been obsessed with her. He would always ask her out on dates or try and fail to woo her with his 'amazing' ninja skills. As if she was interested in him. He was dead last in class and did nothing but pull pranks.

She turned her attention away from the blushing blonde and back towards her crush, "Good morning Sasuke."

He didn't respond back, but acknowledged her greeting with a simple glance. He wasn't very expressive around most people.

"Back off forehead, Sasuke's mine." Ino cut in as she entered the room.

"I was here first." Sakura argued.

"We got here at the same time, remember." Ino reminded.

Before the two could go at it even more all of the Sasuke fangirls seemed to come out of the woodwork. Table after table emptied as more and more girls threw in their claim to Sasuke. The raven-haired boy did his best to tune them all out as he had no interest in any of them. He just wanted to be left alone.

While everyone continued to bicker, the class's resident prankster felt insulted being completely glossed over for the number one student. He took up a position on Sasuke's desk and crouched down to glare at the guy. A glare he reciprocated in kind.

This got the attention of all the girl's present and Sakura herself. "Naruto, stop staring at Sasuke like that!"

He turned towards her with hurt expression, almost like that of a kicked puppy. He quickly shook off that expression however and returned to his glaring match. With each passing second the crowed grew angrier and the two opponents drew closer.

Then something... unexpected happened.

Something that drew the ire of every rookie kunoichi present. Naruto wound up taking all the blame and all the pain of the fangirls. Sakura couldn't do anything from her position, so she was forced to watch as Naruto got pummeled.

After that though, things settled down. Iruka-sensei arrived to assign everyone their genin teams. Much to the pink-haired giantess's joy she was paired with Sasuke, The look on Ino's face was priceless. Then that joy dampened when she also found out she'd be paired with Naruto. Oh yay.

Iruka then announced everyone had an hour to go get lunch and then return to the academy to meet their jonin instructors. "Class dismissed." he said before taking his leave.

Everyone vanished so quick the classroom was like a ghost town. As everyone filed out Sakura found no trace of Sasuke. She waited for a bit but it looked like he avoided everyone all together. She was all alone.

She then stood up. "We're could he have gone? I figured since we're on the same team maybe we could eat lunch together and get to know each other."

"Hey Sakura." A familiar voice called from below. Looking down she could see the small form of Naruto waving to her. Internally she sighed. "Since we're in the same group and all I figured we can get lunch and get to know each other better."
1211.title: Teams

choices: Naruto runs off to the bathroom (reserved)
Naruto and Sakura enjoy a nice ramen lunch
12111.content:
Sakura pinched the bridge of her nose. "Fine."

Naruto actually froze up when he hear the pink-haired giant's reply. Did she basically just say yes? He pinched himself once just be sure he wasn't dreaming, and the brief pain he felt confirmed he was in fact wide awake.

He had just asked his crush out to lunch after striking out for four years, and she finally said yes. Now it was time for the blonde to pump his fist in victory. "Awesome, I know just the spot! Follow me!"

Naruto then started running off with Sakura simply shaking her head at her teammates over abundance of enthusiasm. He was acting as if he'd won the lottery. Why did she have to be partnered with him?

Shaking off her annoyance she followed behind as slowly as possible. Her footsteps actually made him stumble a bit as he hadn't braced himself for the tremors, but quickly recovered.

"Sorry." Sakura apologized. That was one of the biggest downsides to her size, walking next to people meant they had to brave a mini quake.

"It's no problem, Sakura. No harm, no foul." Naruto chuckled. The giantess surprisingly smiled back. Annoying as Naruto could be, he was at least understanding.

Eventually the two managed to maneuver through the village streets until they reached Naruto's favorite spot. An old ramen stand by the name of Ichiraku. At first Sakura had been perplexed that Naruto had chosen such a small little stall, but after hearing him enter and greet the owner she could tell he had a lot of history here. She decided to just sit down next to the stall and wait for her partner to come back. She also hoped she wasn't completely blocking the road with her body.

After a few minutes Naruto came back out with a dopey little grin on his face. "Sakura!? What kind of ramen do you want?"

A good question, she'd never eaten here before or been much of a ramen person. She ate it occasionally but it was never something she actively sought out. "Uh... I'll just have what you're having."

Naruto's smile seemed to brighten. "Nice! Two Miso Ramens then!"

He ran back into the stall while she remained outside. All Sakura could do now was wait and take in the scenery. The village always looked nice this time of day and the bustling streets were always a sight to behold. She did at times wonder what it would be like to be small again for just one day to experience it all the way she used to. It had been so long now that she'd almost forgotten.

It was then Naruto came back out still sporting his fox like grin. "Food will be ready in ten."

Sakura smiled back. "Great."
12111.title: Ichiraku Ramen

choices: Sakura makes it back to the academy (reserved)
121111.content:
For the next five minutes, Naruto and Sakura continued to chat about nothing in particular until the village's resident prankster began to feel an odd tingly sensation in the pit of his stomach.

In seconds he was clutching his stomach and breaking out into a cold sweat. He needed to use the bathroom and he needed to use it now. Without warning he shot up like a rocket and began to run off.

Sakura saw this and was left baffled as her teammate had sudden look off like a bat out of hell. "Hey, Naruto!? Where are you going she called!?"

"I'll be right back!" was all Naruto could get out as he sprinted down the crowded street. Sakura just couldn't figure the guy out. He was finally all alone with her like he always wanted and then ran off. Maybe he was just shy?

Well whatever the reason there wasn't much she could do but wait. "Okay. two orders of Miso Ramen!" came a third voice.

Sakura looked down to see an older man placing two trays of ramen down of the ground in front of her. "Greetings... big lady! Welcome to Ichiraku Ramen." he then looked around briefly. "Where's Naruto? He never misses a ramen call."

Sakura gave the ramen stand owner a kind smile. "He said he'd be right back. Oh, and thank you for the food!"

"No problem. Any friend of Naruto's is a friend of mine, especially when he brings in celebrities." the middle aged man laughed.

Sakura merely blushed at his words. "Please, I'm no celebrity."

"Sure you are, you're Sakura Haruno. Everyone knows you!" The middle aged man smiled. "I have to get back to work now, please enjoy the ramen."

The owner then returned to his booth and left the giant alone in the street with the two orders. To her the tray was ridiculously tiny as well as the bowls. This would never fill her, that much was obvious, but the whole point of getting lunch wasn't about the food. So for now she'd be respectful and wait for Naruto to come back.

----------------------
45 minutes later
----------------------

"That little twerp ditched me." Sakura said to herself. She'd been sitting out in the street waiting like an idiot for the real to come back. The ramen was no doubt cold by now and she'd just about wasted all of her lunchtime on that blonde jerk!

Well he could forget about her be nicer in the future if this was how he was going treat her after trying to give him a chance. Her face scrunched up in an annoyed scowl as she looked down at the cold tray off soups.

As carefully as possible she reached down for a bowl and managed to grip it with her fingernails. Years of practice and failure had taught her how to grasp certain things like bowls with this method, though some small items remain beyond her.

She lifted the bowl to her mouth flipped the contents onto her tongue. She couldn't really taste it too well as it was like a drop to her. She then placed the bowl back down and eyes the second one with annoyance. If Naruto wasn't coming back then he didn't need his food. She then repeated the same action and down his. Again she couldn't really taste it, but she could tell it was cold.

She stood up right after and looked towards the stall. "Thank you for the food, just put it on Naruto's tab."

Without waiting for a reply she walked off in a huff after such a waste of time.
121111.title: Ramen for one

1211111.content:
Sakura sat in silence outside the academy window while her teammates waited inside. At this point in time everyone else had been picked up by their jonin sensei while theirs was late to come collect them.

Sasuke was content to sit at an open desk while Naruto kept peeking into the hall as if that would make their new teacher appear much faster. It was honestly starting to grate on her nerves.

"Naruto! Just sit down!" Sakura scolded.

"I don't want to!" Naruto petulantly replied back. "How come our teacher's the only one that's late! I'm ready to roll, believe it! Besides everyone else already met with their sensei's and went off on some grand adventure or something, even Iruka-sensei's gone too!"

Sakura sighed. As much as she hated to admit it, he kind of had a point. "We get it, okay? Wait!? What are you doing!?"

Naruto was giggling like mad when he pulled up a stool and placed an eraser in the crack of the door.

"Naruto..." Sakura began.

"This is what he gets for showing up late!" Naruto cut off as pulled away the stool. "Boom! Surprise!"

"You're just asking for trouble, you know you shouldn't do that." Sakura chastised, although a huge part of thought their sensei deserved this.

"Our sensei's supposed to be an elite jonin, do you hinestly think he'll fall for that?" Sasuke asked, finally joining the conversation.

"Sasuke's right! You're so clueless Naruto!"

It was then the sound of scraping wood caught their attention. Everyone turned towards the door to silver-haired masked man poke his head in, only to be met head on with the falling eraser. It hit his head with dull *plop* and hit the floor with a wooden thud.

Everyone continued to stare at the scene in silence at what was to come next, that is until Naruto began to laugh his ass off. "Hahahaha! He totally fell for it!"

"I'm sorry sensei, I told him not to do it but he wouldn't listen. I'd never do anything like that!" Sakura quickly said trying to cover her own. Though internally she was pleased the eraser found it's mark.

Sasuke was just silent.

Their sensei ignored their words for a moment, instead focused on picking up the eraser. It was a childish trick that he could have easily avoided. He eyed them all with an unreadable expression. "Hmm? How should I put this. My first impression of this group... I hate you all."

Everyone hung their heads low hearing that. He said it so casually too.

"I'd ask you to meet up on the roof, but given that's not possible for all of you, we'll just have to have our meeting outside." the masked man said before vanishing in a swirl of leaves.

He reappeared outside in front of where Sakura was seated and the giant kunoichi watched him reappear in surprise. He made than shunshin technique look so smooth. Smoother than even Iruka or Mizuki sensei.

"Yo!" he greeted.

Sakura decided right then and there her new sensei was weird.
1211111.title: Meeting the sensei

choices: Sakura tries to ask Sasuke out
12111111.content:
"Alright, why don't you all introduce yourselves. One at a time." said the masked man. From the sound of his voice it was apparent he wasn't really interested in all this.

"Introduce ourselves? Well, what are we supposed to say?" asked Sakura, while she and her teammates eyed the the silver-haired jonin.

"Things you like. Things you hate. Dreams for the future. Hobbies. That sort of thing." the man explained as leaned against a nearby tree.

That made a lot more sense, but still...

"Why don't you go first so we can see how this is supposed to work." Naruto suggested. It was a good idea on his part considering they barely knew anything about the man in front of them.

"Me?" the masked man gestured to himself. "Well I'm Kakashi Hatake. Things I like and things I hate... I don't feel like telling you that. Dreams for the future? Haven't really thought about. As for hobbies, I have lots of hobbies. Now your turn."

"That was completely useless, all he really told us was his name." Sakura whispered to her teammates, although given her size her words weren't really hidden from the jonin.

The now Identified ninja had given them nothing.

Naruto agreed with the pink-haired giant and decided to go first. "Alright, Believe it! I'm Naruto Uzumaki! I like instant ramen in a cup, and I really love the ramen at the Ichiraku noodle shop. I also hate the three minutes you have to wait after you pour the water in the noodle cup..."

It was at this point Sakura began to tune out her teammate as it was clear his mind seemed to revolve around ramen and nothing else. What was so great about ramen anyway? It was just a bunch of empty calories and salt!

"...And my dream is to be the greatest Hokage! That way people will stop disrespecting me and treat me like I'm somebody, somebody important!"

Well that was certainly different compared to all the soup talk. At least the blonde could dream big.

"Alright, you next." Kakashi pointed to Sakura.

The kunochi began to grow slightly nervous but did as instructed. "My name is Sakura Haruno! What I like is..."

Her introduction mostly revolved around her blue-clad teammate. Needless to say like Kakashi her introduction didn't really do much.

"And? Things you hate?" The jonin pushed.

"NARUTO!!!" she said without hesitation. The blonde looked defeated. Poor guy.

"Last one." Kakashi said, trying to speed these introductions along.

"My name is Sauke Uchiha. I hate a lot of things and don't particularly like anything. What I have isn't a dream because I will make it a reality. I will avenge my clan and destroy a certain someone."

The introduction was short, sweet, and to the point. Everyone was silent for a moment as they took in his words. Naruto hoped he wasn't talking about him and Sakura thought the introduction only made her teammate more alluring. Kakashi mentally decided to keep a closer on the Uchiha.

"Good. You're each unique and have your own ideas. We'll have our first mission tomorrow."

"What kind of mission are we going to get sensei?" Naruto asked.

"One that all of us will do together." he answered. "A survival exercise."

"A survival exercise?"

"I thought we were supposed to get a real mission, not more practice." Sakura said slightly annoyed. "We already did this sort of stuff at the academy. That's how we all got here."

"This won't be like your previous training." Kakashi said calmly. Naruto then proceeded to ask what kind of training it would be, which got an ominous laugh out of the jonin.

"That's a normal question, what's so funny?" Sakura asked the chuckling man.

"If I tell you the answer you're not going to like it." Kakashi teased before getting deathly serious. "Of the twenty-seven graduates that just came here only nine will actually be accepted as genin. The other 18 will be weeded out and sent back to the academy. In other words this is a make it or break it pass fail test and the chance that you'll fail is at least sixty-six percent!"

Everyone's blood ran cold.

"See? Didn't I tell you you wouldn't like it." Kakashi finished.

Naruto was the first to recover. "That's just crazy! We worked hard to get here, so what was graduation test for anyway!?"

It became all too clear now to Sakura what that graduation test had been for now. Thankfully she didn't have to explain as the jonin beat her to it. "Oh that? It was just to trim the fat and see which candidates might actually become ninja... or not."

"WHAT!?" Naruto shouted.

"That's just how it is. I decided whether you pass or fail. Be at the designated training spot tomorrow at 5:00am and bring your ninja gear."

Naruto trembled but was determined to not be weeded out. Sakura herself vowed to pass as well. For Sasuke, failure wasn't an option.

"That's it, your all dismissed." Kakashi waved as he was about to take his leave. "Oh!? Before I forget, you might not want eat breakfast unless you want to puke."

After saying that he vanished, leaving the three all alone with their thoughts.
12111111.title: Introductions

choices: Sakura tries to confront Kakashi by herself
Sakura tries her best to hide
121111111.content:
Sakura yawned as she trudged along the dirt path towards training ground three, her mind and body still not ready to fully tackle the day.

Naruto was the same way as the tired blonde stumbled and mumbled his way down the dirt path as well. The poor guy looked like hell.

Sasuke appeared to be fine though. He didn't look all that different from how he usually did during the day. Perhaps he'd chosen to go to bed early?

All three stood next to each other in silence, although Sakura stood head and shoulders above both her squad mates.

Hours passed as dusk became dawn, and dawn became morning. So far there was still no sign of their sensei. Just like back at the academy he was once again late. He was so late in fact that Sakura has taken a seat with Naruto having fallen asleep next to her.

The sun just continued to hang high over the three genin before an all too familiar lazy drawl cut through the silence. "Morning everyone, ready for your first day?"

"You're late!" Naruto and Sakura shouted comically as they sat up and pointed at the man before them.

Kakashi didn't seemed all too affected by their outburst, nor did he question how Naruto had immediately awoken at the sound of his voice. He also didn't bat an eye at Sasuke slightly narrowed pair. Instead he kept things light. "Well a black cat crossed my path and I had to take the long way."

Sakura and Naruto merely growled at the masked ninja's lame excuse. Was he for real!? Just how stupid did he think they were!?

"Well..." he coughed into his hand. "Let's get this started."

The group of three looked on confused as the silver-haired jonin walked over to a lonely stumped and placed an alarm clock in the center.

"Here we go." he began as he set the timer. "It's set for noon." he then dangled a pair of silver bells on a red string in front of them. "Your assignment is very simple, you just have to take these bells from me. See, simple. If you can't get them by noon then you go without lunch."

"Whaa!" Naruto complained.

Kakashi continued without missing a beat and gestured to the three posts off to the side. "You'll all be tied down while I eat my lunch in front of you." he then paused for a moment. "Not you Sakura as that won't be physically possible."

Sakura mentally sighed in relief that her size helped her avoid such an embarrassment. At most he could maybe just tie her ankle to it and that's about it. That's when a thought suddenly occurred to them all, that's why they were told not to eat breakfast. To make the test more difficult.

Sakura was the first to recover from this revelation and took note of something very important. "Wait a minute, there's three of us! So how come there are only two bells?"

Kakashi gave them an eye smile that did nothing to hide the impending horror of his words. "Well this way at least one of you will be tied to a post and ultimately disqualified for failing to complete the mission. That one goes back to the academy. Then again, all three of you could flunk out too. You can use any weapon including shuriken. If you're not prepared to kill me, then you won't get the bells."

"But aren't those weapons too danger sensei!?" Skaura asked worriedly. Not mention she herself could be potentially dangerous given her large stature.

Naruto merely laughed at the situation. "Especially since you couldn't even dodge that eraser!"

"Class clowns are usually the weakest link. It's easier to just ignore them. Lowest scores, losers." Kakashi sniped in his usual lazy manner.

Naruto clearly didn't take that comment to well if his annoyed face was anything to go by.

"When I say start you may begin."

Naruto didn't feel like waiting as before the other's knew it he drew a Kunai and charged the awaiting masked jonin. Sakura and Sasuke gasped at the reckless action, but even more so at what followed... Kakashi right behind Naruto with his own kunai pointed at the back of his head.

"Don't be in such a hurry, I didn't say start yet." Kakashi said to the rash blonde. Naruto hadn't even taken three steps before he'd effectively been shut down.

"So so fast I didn't even see him move!" Sakura thought as she and Sasuke backed up slightly. Kakashi then released the orange-clad genin once he'd calmed down.

"Even still, you came at me fully intent on destroying me, so how can I say this, I'm actually starting to like you guys." Kakashi complimented. "Now get ready... start!"

The three genin scattered.
121111111.title: The test begins

choices: Kakashi rushes off into the woods
1211111111.content:
"A ninja must be able to blend into their environment and hide effectively." Kakashi said absentmindedly. Looking around he was quite pleased to see the genin understood that much, but then he got a look at the tree behind him and paused.

The silver-haired ninja titled his head in confusion at the sight of a tall tree with a rather large body couched down behind it. It was no surprise Sakura was having difficulty hiding to her size, but he still expected at least a little bit more effort than that.

Sakura seemed to feel his eyes on her and peaked around the tree to see him staring right at her. Right then and there she knew the jig was up as her body wouldn't allow her to hide properly. One of the downsides of being a giant.

The giantess sighed and stood up from her hiding spot, instead opting to walk out into the open where her sensei was waiting. The clearing itself was rather large and there was a small lake off to the side.

"I take it hiding isn't necessarily your strong suit." Kakashi commented as he met the gaze of the girl that easily towered over him and everyone else in the village.

The pinkette blushed in embarrassment at his words, her eyes falling to her feet. "Sorry sensei. Hiding's not that easy for me."

"I guess not." he brushed off. Sakura's inability to hide effectively would be a problem, but right now he wasn't too worried about that. There were other skills that he needed to evaluate. "Well then, I guess you'll just have to come at me then and try to take a bell for yourself. With your size that shouldn't be much of a problem."

Sakura looked uneasy hearing that. Her size was as much a curse as it was a boon, and against another human being she could easily hurt someone. "But sensei, I could accidently hurt you if I'm not careful!"

"Oh? Then I suppose you'd rather fail instead then?" Kakashi teased.

Sakura winced.

Kakashi continued on. "That's what'll happen if you're not willing to hurt me to get what you need. I know that your size easily makes you more dangerous than any kunai or shuriken, but these are the stake... you either pass, or fail!"

Sakura's eyes widened.

"If your not prepared to kill me, then you won't get the bells." he finished.

The pink-haired giant tightened her fists and did her best to glare at the jonin. He didn't need to remind her anymore what would happen should she fail in her objective. Failure meant returning to the academy, failure meant Ino winning their rivalry, failure meant no more Sasuke!

She couldn't allow herself to fail here! If a fight is what Kakashi-sensei wanted, then a fight he would get! Sakura took a basic academy fighting stance and narrowed her eyes at the 'little' man.

"Shinobi battle tactics part one, taijutsu, the physical part. "Kakashi said before getting into his own stance. Now it was time to truly evaluate his genin.
1211111111.title: Confrontation

choices: Kakashi and the bells vanish as Sakura was in a genjutsu
12111111111.content:
Kakashi eyed the giant kunoichi with a frightening degree of calm, although on the inside he did feel an ever so slight ping of nervousness. He'd been on many missions in his long career as a shinobi and fought many opponents bigger than him, but never had he faced someone quite this big.

Sakura Haruno was 52ft tall and weight in at 40tons if he remembered correctly. There was no conceivable way for him to get her off her feet on his own and trying to outmuscle her was just laughable. To be quite honest his options were extremely limited.

He could always use... THAT! No, no it was far too early to rely on his trump card. Especially against mere genin. Though, Sakura didn't really count as a mere genin. Oh well, he didn't need to really win this fight anyway, just test these kids. So with that in mind he decided to engage.

"Well? Lady's first." Kakashi beckoned with his hand.

Sakura obliged him and ran forward. Kakashi braced himself for the giant's attack and was momentarily surprised when she broke off her run and jumped high into the air. The silver-haired man put two-and-two together and quickly realized her intent. She was going for an air stomp!

Meanwhile in their respective hiding spots both Naruto and Sasuke's eyes widened when they too realized what their teammate was going to do.

Sasuke began to make tracks as he sprinted as far as he could from the battle zone and Naruto swiftly made himself a protective walls of clones.

"Earth style, mud wall!" Kakashi shouted as a dome of earth surrounded him.

It did very little as the pink meteor made contact with the earth. The resounding impact was like that of a explosion. The ground shook apart, trees were uprooted, and her teammates were sent flying. Sasuke was sent souring through the air like a bird and Naruto's clones did nothing to prevent him from flying off and into the next training ground.

When the dust settled there was nothing but Sakura knelt down in a large crater holding a shocked and battered Kakashi Hatake in her grasp. He hadn't expected a genin to already have such fine chakra control that her attack could cause this much damage.

The training ground wasn't even recognizable anymore and he was caught. Sakura completely obliterated his defenses and won the battle in one move. Maybe he should've used his trump card.

He then felt something being ripped from his belt. "The bells." he commented, sounding exhausted.

"I win." Sakura smiled.

"Yeah." Kakashi sighed. "You really did."
12111111111.title: Cherry blossom victory

121111111111.content:
Both Naruto and Sasuke grumbled as the two were unceremoniously tied to posts. Somehow the damned hunks of wood survived the devastation inflicted by their 52ft teammate.

Sasuke looked a battered from his landing and Naruto wasn't much better. Sasuke had his fall cushioned by tree branches and ground while Naruto's landed involved him and rock.

To the scuffed up jonin watching the pair, the two looked like they'd been in a bar fight and came out with all kinds of cuts and bruises. The only differences being they weren't badges of honor.

To Sakura who was seated off the side, her teammates looked like they'd been put through the wringer. She felt a pang of guilt for them as she knew her chakra enhanced attack was the cause.

"Sorry." she muttered. Naruto kindly brushed off her attack as no big deal while Sasuke mumbled something under his breath. She hoped he wasn't too mad at her.

"Well? Where do I even begin." Kakashi sighed, this test had gone far differently then he'd planned, though he admittedly didn't have much of a plan going in. "Your team is the very one to ever get the bells from me, congratulations."

Sakura felt a bit of pride swell up inside her and so did Naruto even though he didn't really do much. Sasuke however remained silent and just listened.

"So Sakura?" Kakshi asked the pink-haired giant. "Who here advances to the next level, and how gets sent back down the latter?"

"Huh!?" she says in surprise. "What do you mean?"

"You retrieved both bells from me so it's only fitting you decide who passes with you and who fails." the jonin explained.

Hearing that Naruto began to grow nervous and Sasuke began to eye his giant teammate critically.

"Oh! That's easy..." she said confidently before pausing. She looked at her two teammates and took note of how they watching her. Sasuke's was a lot harder to read and Naruto looked more nervous than she'd ever seen him. Earlier she had listen to each of her teammates describe their goals and now it was time kill one of those dreams.

Sasuke was Sasuke so she couldn't send him back, but Naruto? His dream was to reach the top and be someone important. It was childish sure, but everyone wants to be the best. And what was it he said earlier about people disrespecting him?

Then there was her sensei who'd taken this time to take a seat himself and let her choose. Jerk! How was she supposed to do this. She couldn't be asked to rub out someone's dreams.

"Crap!" she said. "Sasuke and Naruto can have both bells. I'll go back!"

Everyone looked floored.

"No! You can't go back Sasukra, you worked too hard!" Naruto tried to encourage. "Just send me back! What's one more year, right!"

"No." she sighed. "I can't crush your dream. I can't crush anybody's dreams."

"Good answer." Kakashi finally cut in. "You pass!"

"What!?" She said plainly.

"You all pass." Kakashi repeated. "The other teams I had in the past didn't show even a hint of what you did here today. To put the team ahead of yourselves is what the true purpose of the bell test is. Those who break the rules are scum, but those who would betray one of their friends are even worse than scum. The test is over. Team 7 starts it's first mission tomorrow!"

Naruto cried tears of Joy while Sasuke smirked to himself. Sakura was in the same boat as Naruto with tears of of Joy.

"Let's all go home!" Kakashi said as he and Sakura began to leave. It was then Naruto and Sasuke remembered they were still tied up.

"Hey!" Sasuke struggled.

"Don't leave me here with him!" Naruto complained.

"Shut it loser!"

"You need to untie me!"
121111111111.title: You pass
